ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn code exists for this specific restaurant salad composition. FOODON_00002539 (salad food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Salads) is the closest parent category. No specific code for romaine-avocado-almond salad with honey mustard dressing.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (Salad) used as closest parent; no specific code for this restaurant salad variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of Riverside Salad is approximately 300 g, comprising a full plate of romaine lettuce, tomato, Spanish onion, olives, cucumber, avocado, almonds, and honey mustard dressing.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Salad, composed of romaine lettuce, avocado, tomato, cucumber, onion, olives, nuts, and salad dressing (composite estimate)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry matches this exact restaurant salad. Values estimated by ingredient-weighted composite using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for romaine lettuce (FDC 169247), tomato (FDC 170457), cucumber (FDC 168409), avocado (FDC 171705), almonds (FDC 170567), olives (FDC 169094), onion (FDC 170000), and a honey mustard dressing analogue (FDC 172920). Proportions estimated from typical restaurant salad composition: romaine 30%, tomato 12%, cucumber 10%, avocado 15%, almonds 8%, olives 5%, onion 5%, dressing 15%. Per-100g values derived from weighted average of these components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed salad with vegetables and dressing analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific restaurant salad. GI estimated at 32 based on analogous mixed green salad with dressing entries from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). Romaine lettuce, cucumber, tomato, and avocado are very low GI vegetables (GI 10–20); almonds have GI ~0; honey mustard dressing contributes modest sugar. Composite GI estimated at 32 (low). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (32 × 8.4 × 3.0) / 100 = 8.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from olives and honey mustard dressing
hypertension.guidance: The Riverside Salad contains approximately 210 mg sodium per 100 g (630 mg per 300 g serving), primarily from olives and honey mustard dressing; individuals with hypertension should request dressing on the side and limit olive portions to keep sodium within daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and high dietary fibre from vegetables and avocado
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 32 and 3.1 g dietary fibre per 100 g, this salad produces a low glycemic load and supports postprandial blood glucose control; the honey mustard dressing contributes modest sugar (~3.8 g per 100 g) and portion control of the dressing is advisable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High monounsaturated fat from avocado and almonds; low saturated fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The avocado and almond content provide heart-healthy monounsaturated fats (6.8 g per 100 g) and dietary fibre, supporting LDL cholesterol reduction; saturated fat is low at 1.4 g per 100 g, making this salad a cardioprotective meal choice when dressing quantity is moderated.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Riverside Salad is a composed fresh salad served at Riverside Buns, a Taiwanese-style café-restaurant located at Boat Quay, Singapore. The salad consists of romaine lettuce, fresh tomato, Spanish onion, green olives, cucumber, ripe avocado, toasted almonds, and honey mustard dressing. It is assembled raw and served cold, making it a minimally processed dish. The texture profile combines the crisp crunch of romaine and cucumber, the creaminess of avocado, the nuttiness of almonds, and the tangy-sweet flavour of honey mustard dressing. Nutritionally, the salad is rich in monounsaturated fats from avocado and almonds, provides moderate dietary fibre (3.1 g per 100 g), and contains vitamins A, C, E, and folate from the vegetable components. The glycemic index is low (approximately 32), making it suitable for blood glucose management. Sodium is moderate at approximately 210 mg per 100 g due to olives and dressing. The dish is vegetarian, dairy-free, and halal-suitable. It is priced at SGD 16.00 and represents a Western-style café salad within the Singapore food-service context. Clinically, it is appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease risk, or weight management, provided dressing portions are controlled.
common_user_queries: What is in the Riverside Salad?
Is the Riverside Salad suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in Riverside Salad?
What dressing is on the Riverside Salad?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific restaurant salad in any approved primary source. Values are estimated from a weighted ingredient composite using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for each component. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database for mixed vegetable salads. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient list is fully specified and the USDA component data is high quality.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted ingredient composite (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy) based on the declared ingredient list: romaine lettuce, tomato, Spanish onion, olives, cucumber, avocado, almonds, honey mustard dressing. Estimated proportions: romaine 30%, tomato 12%, cucumber 10%, avocado 15%, almonds 8%, olives 5%, onion 5%, dressing 15%. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based salad with no hydrogenated fats). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients contributing B12). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin D-containing ingredients present; plant foods do not contribute meaningful vitamin D). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no animal-derived ingredients in this salad). The cuisine tag 'Taiwanese' in the input record reflects the restaurant brand identity (Riverside Buns is a Taiwanese-style café); the salad itself is a Western-style composed salad. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules as the food is served in Singapore. Serving size estimated at 300 g based on typical restaurant salad portion. GI of 32 is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is not available. Sodium level is moderate and primarily attributable to olives and honey mustard dressing. Allergens include tree nuts (almonds) and mustard. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA component data and are not -1 sentinel values.
